title: pteridium aquilinum var. esculentum
intro: Pteridium aquilinum var. esculentum, also known as bracken fern, is a variety of the pteridium aquilinum plant, a type of fern that grows in many parts of the world, particularly in temperate and subtropical regions. The bracken fern has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing the risk of certain cancers, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive health. Bracken fern is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be carefully considered due to potential toxicity and side effects.
